Transition Stewardship Agreement Summary
Plain-Language Overview for Incoming CEO & Trustee
Company
{{companyName}}

Engagement Reference
{{ref}}
§ 01Who Is Forhemit?

Forhemit Stewardship Management Co. is a California Public Benefit Corporation that facilitates employee ownership transitions. Forhemit coordinated the ESOP transaction that brought your company to 100% employee ownership.

Forhemit is not a buyer, investor, lender, broker, attorney, or financial advisor. Forhemit does not hold equity in the company, does not sit on the board, and does not make management decisions.

§ 02What Does Forhemit Do Post-Close?

Forhemit provides 12 to 24 months of operational stewardship support after the ESOP closes. Think of Forhemit as your experienced ESOP operations partner during the transition:

01Continuity of Operations Plan (COOP) — Maintains and updates the operational playbook developed during the transaction, ensuring nothing falls through the cracks.
02ESOP Administration Support — Coordinates with the trustee, plan administrator, and counsel on annual compliance, repurchase obligation planning, and participant communications.
03Financial Monitoring — Provides quarterly status reports on ESOP health metrics, debt service coverage, and repurchase obligation projections.
04Governance Advisory — Serves as a resource for the board and CEO on ESOP governance best practices, fiduciary considerations, and employee-ownership culture.
05Lender & Trustee Coordination — Acts as the primary point of contact for ongoing SBA lender reporting and trustee communication, reducing the CEO's administrative burden.
§ 03What Does Forhemit NOT Do?

Forhemit does not:

• Make business decisions or manage employees

• Provide legal advice or act as legal counsel

• Provide tax advice or prepare tax returns

• Serve as an ERISA fiduciary

• Hold equity or voting rights in the company

• Receive any share of seller proceeds

§ 04How Much Does Stewardship Cost?
{{annualStewardshipFee}}
{{stewardshipRate}} of company EBITDA, per engagement agreement
{{billingFrequency}} in arrears
Ordinary business expense — fully deductible
Key Point: This fee is paid by the company as a business expense. It does not come out of employee distributions, does not reduce the ESOP trust's assets, and does not affect the seller's note.
§ 05How Long Does Stewardship Last?

The stewardship period is typically 12 to 24 months, depending on the complexity of the transition. Either party may end the stewardship with 30 days' written notice.

At the end of stewardship, Forhemit provides a final summary and hands off all documentation, contacts, and institutional knowledge to the company's leadership team.

§ 06Key Contacts
